steps.title: Use the hive-building pattern
steps.content: The passage says a larger size difference between worker and drone cells creates a bigger construction problem, so bees use more nonhexagonal cells to fill gaps. The graph shows western honeybees have far fewer nonhexagonal cells than the other two species.

steps.title: Infer the size difference
steps.content: Choice A follows because fewer nonhexagonal cells suggest worker and drone cells are closer in size for western honeybees. Choice B assigns cell types without support. Choice C contradicts the simpler shape pattern. Choice D reverses the hexagonal-cell implication. The answer is A.
